Default_value_for_agent: ≈0.49 mΩ/square at 20°C; R = Rsq·length/width
Range_or_sensitivity: 0.25 mΩ/sq for 2 oz, ≈0.98 mΩ/sq for 0.5 oz; +0.393%/°C
Formula_or_method: Rsq=ρ/t; R=ρ·l/(w·t)
Use_when: First-pass supply/ground voltage-drop and damping estimates
LTspice_representation: Rser in series with trace segment

Structure: Single copper trace without close return plane
Default_value_for_agent: Use 0.6–1.2 nH/mm as rough upper-bound; isolated formula preferred
Range_or_sensitivity: Strongly geometry-dependent; wide traces lower L but return path dominates
Use_when: When no plane/return path is close or for conservative ringing estimate

Structure: 50Ω microstrip/stripline trace with adjacent return plane
Range_or_sensitivity: Lower for low-Z wide traces; higher for high-Z narrow traces
Formula_or_method: L'=Z0·td; use propagation delay td from stack-up or calculator
Use_when: High-speed signal or power loop over continuous plane

Range_or_sensitivity: Depends on Er, soldermask and geometry
Formula_or_method: C'=td/Z0

Default_value_for_agent: ≈3.95 nH/cm and ≈0.70 pF/cm in TI example
Range_or_sensitivity: Example given for εr≈3.8; use only as sanity check
Formula_or_method: L'=Z0·td; C'=td/Z0
Use_when: Model long traces/cables when rise time is short

Default_value_for_agent: ≈1.8 nH/cm and ≈1.47 pF/cm in TI example
Range_or_sensitivity: Example given for εr≈2.4; exact board differs
Formula_or_method: L'=Z0·td; C'=td/Z0
Use_when: Compare low-impedance power/signal traces over planes

Default_value_for_agent: C[pF]=0.008854·εr·A_mm²/d_mm
Range_or_sensitivity: Example: 100×100mm, d=0.1mm, εr=4.2 → ≈3.7nF
Formula_or_method: Parallel plate approximation
Use_when: PDN/plane capacitance and HF decoupling estimate
LTspice_representation: C between VCC and GND planes

Default_value_for_agent: L[nH]≈1.257·d_mm·(length/width) per rectangular current path
Range_or_sensitivity: For 0.1mm dielectric ≈0.126 nH/square
Formula_or_method: Parallel-plate inductance approximation
LTspice_representation: Lser in supply loop or plane segment

Default_value_for_agent: C≈8.85fF/mm²·εr/d_mm; keep area small
Range_or_sensitivity: Large SW area injects displacement current; risk rises with dv/dt
Formula_or_method: I=C·dv/dt; compute C to all nearby conductors
LTspice_representation: C from SW node to planes/chassis/primary/secondary

Structure: Large quiet copper pour near noisy node
Default_value_for_agent: Estimate as parallel-plate upper bound; reduce by distance/guard/shield
Range_or_sensitivity: Coupling proportional to parallel area and inverse spacing
Formula_or_method: C=εA/d; Inoise=C·dv/dt

Structure: Trace crossing split/slot in reference plane
Default_value_for_agent: Avoid; if unavoidable add return stitching near crossing
Range_or_sensitivity: Can dominate over trace inductance; hard to estimate without geometry
Formula_or_method: Use longer return path loop-area estimate; L∝loop area for rough comparison
Use_when: Any fast edge or high di/dt trace crossing gaps
LTspice_representation: Additional L in return and mutual coupling source

Structure: Signal with continuous adjacent GND plane
Default_value_for_agent: Use TL L′ from impedance/delay; do not use isolated trace formula
Range_or_sensitivity: Return current follows under trace at high frequency
Formula_or_method: L'=Z0·td
Use_when: Fast digital, clocks, gate drive, sensor lines
LTspice_representation: Distributed TL or small L loop

Default_value_for_agent: Expect substantially higher and layout-sensitive L than 4-layer plane
Range_or_sensitivity: Use ground grid + short returns; via stitching helps
Formula_or_method: Estimate loop area; compare variants by A reduction

Default_value_for_agent: ≈0.8–1.5 nH per via
Range_or_sensitivity: Shorter/thicker vias lower L; multiple vias in parallel reduce L

Structure: Power/GND via pair for decoupling capacitor
Default_value_for_agent: Use 0.5–2 nH for via+pad+escape if not extracted
Range_or_sensitivity: Via spacing and path geometry dominate; place P/G vias close together
Formula_or_method: Use via L + pad/trace L; reduce by parallel vias

Default_value_for_agent: L≈Lsingle/N only if close and sharing current well
Range_or_sensitivity: Mutual coupling limits perfect 1/N scaling
Formula_or_method: Use Lparallel≈Lsingle/(0.6N to N) as conservative range
LTspice_representation: Parallel L elements or reduced L

Default_value_for_agent: ≈0.1–0.6 pF typical; formula gives first estimate
Range_or_sensitivity: Can matter for fast I/O, RF, filter leakage
Formula_or_method: C[pF]≈0.0555·εr·T_mm·Dpad_mm/(Dantipad_mm-Dpad_mm)

Default_value_for_agent: Treat as open stub; avoid if edge rates demand
Range_or_sensitivity: Quarter-wave stub resonance may notch high-speed signals
Formula_or_method: f≈v/(4·length); use εeff/εr propagation
LTspice_representation: Open transmission-line stub or C/L lump

Default_value_for_agent: ≈0.2–0.7 nH including good pads/vias
Range_or_sensitivity: Actual ESL depends on package and mounting loop; use S-parameters when available
Formula_or_method: ESL = intrinsic cap ESL + mounting loop L

Default_value_for_agent: ≈0.5–1.5 nH if layout is decent
Range_or_sensitivity: Large loop can exceed component ESL
Formula_or_method: Use resonance f=1/(2π√(LC)) to fit ESL from impedance data

Default_value_for_agent: Add ≈0.3 nH/mm if routed over plane; more without plane
Range_or_sensitivity: Distance can dominate capacitor usefulness above tens of MHz
Formula_or_method: Ltrace≈Z0·td·l or rule-of-thumb nH/mm
Use_when: When judging whether cap is local or bulk

Structure: Capacitor with one via far from the other
Default_value_for_agent: Use +0.5–2 nH penalty vs paired close vias
Range_or_sensitivity: Current loop area grows; ESL increases
Formula_or_method: Estimate via-pair loop with trace length + via L

Default_value_for_agent: Few nF possible only with large area and thin dielectric
Range_or_sensitivity: Do not expect planes alone to decouple low frequency
Formula_or_method: C=ε0εrA/d

Default_value_for_agent: Ztarget=ΔV/Itransient
Range_or_sensitivity: Use current step and allowed ripple; then place poles/anti-resonances below target
Formula_or_method: Z=V/I; capacitor impedance includes ESR/ESL

Default_value_for_agent: Expect impedance peaking between SRFs; add damping ESR/ferrite if needed
Range_or_sensitivity: Ideal parallel caps can mislead; include ESR/ESL
Formula_or_method: Simulate each C with ESR/ESL, sweep impedance

Default_value_for_agent: Aim <1–3 nH for compact layouts; estimate with via+trace+package L
Range_or_sensitivity: Higher L causes VIN ringing and emissions
Formula_or_method: Sum trace/via/package L; fit from ringing if measured

Default_value_for_agent: Model Csw ≈ area-to-plane capacitance; minimize copper area
Range_or_sensitivity: Displacement current I=C·dv/dt can be large for fast edges
Formula_or_method: C=εA/d, I=C·dv/dt

Default_value_for_agent: Use 0.5–3 nH as first-pass depending on trace/via path
Range_or_sensitivity: Can cause gate ringing and EMI; route gate and return together
Formula_or_method: L≈trace L + via L; validate via ringing
Inputs_needed: gate trace length, return, resistor placement
LTspice_representation: Lser in gate and source return

Default_value_for_agent: Keep pair tight; model pickup as V=M·di/dt if near power loop
Range_or_sensitivity: Sense signal often <100mV; susceptible to di/dt noise
Formula_or_method: M=k√(L1L2), or empirical injected source
LTspice_representation: Voltage source coupled to sense input

Structure: Snubber loop from SW to GND/VIN
Default_value_for_agent: Assume 0.5–2 nH if tight; poor placement can invalidate snubber
Range_or_sensitivity: Snubber must sit at source of ringing current
Formula_or_method: Fit L/C from ringing: Cpar=1/((2πf)^2L) or use two-cap method

Default_value_for_agent: SMPS transformer can be tens to hundreds pF; Würth slides note up to 1000pF as possible coupling
Range_or_sensitivity: Dominant CM conducted-emissions path in offline supplies
Formula_or_method: Use measured/vendor interwinding C or estimate geometry
LTspice_representation: Cpri-sec between switching node/primary and secondary/PE

Default_value_for_agent: Keep to chassis/PE return short; model ESL + trace L
Range_or_sensitivity: Lead/trace inductance reduces HF effectiveness
Formula_or_method: Add 0.5–5 nH based on path length
LTspice_representation: C with series ESL and ESR

Structure: Mains input traces / fuse / NTC loops
Default_value_for_agent: Use wide/short paths; add trace L by geometry for high-frequency model
Range_or_sensitivity: Safety spacing may force loops; model for EMI only, not safety compliance
Formula_or_method: Ltrace+wire L; include LISN source impedance

Default_value_for_agent: Slots reduce capacitance but may force return path detours
Range_or_sensitivity: Good for isolation, can worsen loop area if poorly placed
Formula_or_method: Compute reduced overlap C; add return inductance detour

Default_value_for_agent: Use 0.5–0.8 µH/m and 50–120 pF/m if no datasheet
Range_or_sensitivity: Actual cable can differ; shield changes capacitance
Formula_or_method: Prefer datasheet; otherwise TL formulas from Z0 and velocity

Default_value_for_agent: 0.59 µH/m and 89.2 pF/m conductor-to-conductor
Range_or_sensitivity: Use only as example for similar shielded twisted pair
Formula_or_method: Use datasheet values directly

Default_value_for_agent: Approx. L≈250 nH/m, C≈100 pF/m
Range_or_sensitivity: Use actual cable datasheet; velocity factor changes values
Formula_or_method: For lossless line: L'=Z0/v, C'=1/(Z0·v)

Default_value_for_agent: ≈5–15 nH/cm as a rough pigtail inductance
Range_or_sensitivity: Pigtail becomes ineffective at high frequency; 360° bond preferred
Formula_or_method: Wire/pair inductance estimate; compare XL=2πfL

Default_value_for_agent: ≈0.1 pF/cm from Würth slide example
Range_or_sensitivity: Illustrative only; dielectric and geometry matter
Formula_or_method: Use line/cable calculator or datasheet

Default_value_for_agent: ≈10 nH/cm from Würth slide example
Range_or_sensitivity: Illustrative; loop geometry controls inductance
Formula_or_method: Use loop inductance / cable calculator

Default_value_for_agent: Treat as TL if one-way delay > ~tr/6 to tr/10
Range_or_sensitivity: Faster edges matter more than clock frequency
Formula_or_method: td = length / velocity; compare to edge rise time
LTspice_representation: Transmission line instead of lumped C

Default_value_for_agent: ≈140–170 ps/in (≈55–67 ps/cm) typical
Range_or_sensitivity: Depends on εeff; stripline often slower
Formula_or_method: td≈√εeff/c; or calculator

Default_value_for_agent: ≈160–190 ps/in (≈63–75 ps/cm) typical
Range_or_sensitivity: Depends on εr and material; exact stack-up needed
Formula_or_method: td≈√εr/c

Default_value_for_agent: Trace adds ≈0.1pF/mm for ~50Ω line
Range_or_sensitivity: Long stubs can dominate edge rate/overshoot
Formula_or_method: C'=td/Z0

Default_value_for_agent: Do not estimate from single-ended only when spacing is small
Range_or_sensitivity: Use calculator/field solver; differential impedance and skew matter
Formula_or_method: Use 2D solver or Saturn/Sierra tool

Default_value_for_agent: Place at source; value ≈Z0−Rdriver
Range_or_sensitivity: Also reduces edge rate and EMI
Formula_or_method: Use reflection model; Rseries chosen against Z0

Default_value_for_agent: Model interwinding C and asymmetry if >10s MHz
Range_or_sensitivity: Layout imbalance converts DM↔CM
Formula_or_method: Use vendor S-parameters; add C across windings if simplified

Default_value_for_agent: Coupling increases with parallel length and decreases with spacing
Range_or_sensitivity: Use 3W spacing as coarse routing heuristic; field solver for exact C
Formula_or_method: Ccpl proportional to parallel length / spacing; calculator preferred

Default_value_for_agent: Reduce loop area and increase distance; M=k√(L1L2)
Range_or_sensitivity: k can vary from <0.01 to >0.3 by geometry
Formula_or_method: Use mutual coupling coefficient in LTspice K statement

Structure: Trace/polygon broadside overlap on adjacent layers
Default_value_for_agent: C[pF]=0.008854·εr·A_mm²/d_mm; use as upper bound
Range_or_sensitivity: Fringing and soldermask affect result
Formula_or_method: Parallel-plate formula

Structure: Digital line near analog high impedance node
Default_value_for_agent: I=Ccouple·dv/dt; even 0.1pF can matter with ns edges
Range_or_sensitivity: High impedance converts small current to voltage
Formula_or_method: V≈I·Zvictim

Structure: Power return shared by signal return
Default_value_for_agent: Vnoise=Inoise·Zcommon
Range_or_sensitivity: Even mΩ/nH matter at high di/dt
Formula_or_method: Z=R+jωL; V=I·Z

Default_value_for_agent: Expect ±10% unless fabricator controls tightly; specify stack-up/coupon
Range_or_sensitivity: Calculator result is not manufactured result
Formula_or_method: Use fab stack-up, TDR coupon, tolerance model

Default_value_for_agent: Use finished copper thickness, not nominal foil only
Range_or_sensitivity: Outer layers plated; etch tolerance changes width
Formula_or_method: Ask fabricator for finished trace width/thickness

Default_value_for_agent: Can lower impedance a few percent by increasing effective ε
Range_or_sensitivity: Depends on mask thickness and coverage
Formula_or_method: Use coated microstrip calculator or fab data

Default_value_for_agent: Avoid thermal relief on high-current/HF decoupling paths where assembly allows
Range_or_sensitivity: Thermal spokes add R and L
Formula_or_method: Model each spoke as narrow trace segments

Structure: Via in pad / filled via for decoupling or BGA
Default_value_for_agent: Can reduce escape inductance and loop area
Range_or_sensitivity: Cost/process impact; fab DFM required
Formula_or_method: Compare shorter path and via count

Default_value_for_agent: Prefer signal next to solid GND plane for EMC/SI
Range_or_sensitivity: Power plane can be return only if well-decoupled to GND at relevant frequency
Formula_or_method: Use TL calculator; avoid split returns

Parasitic: PDN capacitance and low spreading inductance
Default_value_for_agent: Use thin dielectric 50–150µm where possible for HF benefit
Range_or_sensitivity: Capacitance still small vs MLCCs; inductance benefit is key
Formula_or_method: C=εA/d, L=nH/sq approx

Default_value_for_agent: Better shielding than microstrip; slower propagation and higher capacitance
Range_or_sensitivity: Via transitions add discontinuities
Formula_or_method: Use stripline calculator

Structure: Any capacitor intended for HF filtering
Parasitic: Do not model as ideal C
Default_value_for_agent: Always include ESR, ESL, and layout L for >1 MHz EMI work
Range_or_sensitivity: Ideal C can hide resonance/anti-resonance
Formula_or_method: Series R-L-C; fit from impedance curve
Use_when: Conducted EMI filter or decoupling simulation

Parasitic: Do not model as pure inductance
Default_value_for_agent: Use impedance-vs-frequency or vendor SPICE/S-parameter model
Range_or_sensitivity: DC bias and current alter impedance
Formula_or_method: Use vendor model, or frequency-dependent R/L ladder

Default_value_for_agent: Include winding capacitance and ESR/DCR
Range_or_sensitivity: Above SRF, inductor behaves capacitive
Formula_or_method: Use vendor impedance model or add Cp parallel

Default_value_for_agent: Model LISN and split CM/DM currents if possible
Range_or_sensitivity: Wrong mode leads to wrong filter choice
Formula_or_method: Use probes/current sums/differences

Default_value_for_agent: f=5033/sqrt(L_nH·C_pF) MHz
Range_or_sensitivity: Need one of L or C or use two-cap method
Formula_or_method: Use added cap to solve original C and L

Default_value_for_agent: Q and damping from waveform envelope; choose snubber to lower Q
Range_or_sensitivity: Measurement probe inductance can distort result
Formula_or_method: Fit time-domain model with parasitics

Structure: Kicad/Altium geometry known but no solver
Default_value_for_agent: Break long traces into 5–20mm sections for EMI band; use TL for fast nets
Range_or_sensitivity: Too coarse hides resonances; too fine burdens SPICE
Formula_or_method: Segment L/R/C per length

Structure: Large polygon carrying DC and HF current
Default_value_for_agent: R=Rsq·number_of_squares; split current paths where needed
Range_or_sensitivity: Polygon shape/current crowding can invalidate single path
Formula_or_method: Approximate with rectangles; use PEEC/field solver for critical

Default_value_for_agent: Treat as antenna/capacitive plate; keep noisy polygons away from edges/connectors
Range_or_sensitivity: Geometry and cable dominates; use measurements later
Formula_or_method: Compute C to cable/chassis and loop length fraction of wavelength

Default_value_for_agent: Model capacitor ESL plus path to chassis/return; placement can change attenuation by >10 dB in HF
Range_or_sensitivity: Ground connection geometry dominates
Formula_or_method: Use measured insertion loss or layout parasitics
Inputs_needed: cap package, path length, chassis bond

Default_value_for_agent: Include capacitor ESR, inductor DCR, source/load impedance
Range_or_sensitivity: Undamped filter can ring and worsen emissions
Formula_or_method: Check poles/Q; add damping R/RC if needed

Default_value_for_agent: At high frequency CM choke attenuation can collapse through capacitance
Range_or_sensitivity: Use vendor impedance/S-parameter; add Cp as fallback
Formula_or_method: Cp from datasheet or fit to SRF

Default_value_for_agent: Keep <few nH; every nH adds V=L·di/dt during ESD
Range_or_sensitivity: Even short traces can cause large voltage during fast ESD
Formula_or_method: V=L·di/dt; estimate L by length/via

Default_value_for_agent: Use multiple short stitching vias/bonds; avoid long pigtail
Range_or_sensitivity: Long bond degrades HF shield connection
Formula_or_method: XL=2πfL for proposed L

Structure: Input RC filter trace and ground
Default_value_for_agent: Model C to nearby digital/SW nodes if high impedance
Range_or_sensitivity: pF-level coupling can disturb high-Z nodes
Formula_or_method: I=C·dv/dt, V=I·Zinput

Default_value_for_agent: Add 0.1–2 pF coupling if routed near SW node as sensitivity test
Range_or_sensitivity: Feedback node high impedance, noise modulates duty cycle
Formula_or_method: C=εA/d upper bound; sweep Ccouple

Default_value_for_agent: Route supply/return together; estimate loop L from geometry
Range_or_sensitivity: Large loop radiates and picks up; cable adds L
Formula_or_method: Use loop inductance/cable per length

Default_value_for_agent: Use 0.1–1 pF as rough coupling sensitivity if no data
Range_or_sensitivity: Depends on connector geometry, plastic, shields
Formula_or_method: Use datasheet/S-parameters where possible

Default_value_for_agent: Place within a few mm of signal via for fast edges; model added return via L if far
Range_or_sensitivity: Without return via, return current detours
Formula_or_method: Estimate loop from signal via to return via distance

Default_value_for_agent: For RF shielding use spacing << λ/10 in dielectric; for EMC, denser near noisy edges/connectors
Range_or_sensitivity: Not a substitute for solid plane
Formula_or_method: spacing relative to highest relevant frequency

Default_value_for_agent: Neck dominates R/L; model narrowest section, not whole pour
Range_or_sensitivity: Useful for fuses/sense but bad for HF current
Formula_or_method: R=Rsq·squares; L from trace width/length

Default_value_for_agent: Ensure local decoupling capacitor close to transition; model cap ESL
Range_or_sensitivity: Return jumps through decap impedance at high frequency
Formula_or_method: Use decap RLC between planes at transition

Default_value_for_agent: Keep high dv/dt/di/dt traces away from edges/connectors; add guard ground
Range_or_sensitivity: Edge proximity increases fringe fields and cable coupling
Formula_or_method: Model C to chassis/cable as sensitivity

Default_value_for_agent: Use 0.05–0.3 pF per small pad to plane as sensitivity test
Range_or_sensitivity: Pad capacitance matters at RF/fast nodes
Formula_or_method: C=εA/d using pad area

Default_value_for_agent: Model pad-to-plane and pad-to-pad C for EMI filter/SW node
Range_or_sensitivity: Can bypass inductor at high frequency
Formula_or_method: C=εA/d; vendor model preferred
LTspice_representation: Cp across L and C to planes

Default_value_for_agent: Sweep at least ×0.5/×1/×2; for uncertain coupling use decade sweep
Range_or_sensitivity: Single guessed value can mislead
Formula_or_method: Use .step param in LTspice

Default_value_for_agent: Use conservative ranges, not exact values; request stack-up/geometry
Range_or_sensitivity: Agent should not present exact pass/fail
Formula_or_method: Use known generic defaults and document assumptions

Structure: Frequency above ~100–300 MHz on FR-4 PCB
Default_value_for_agent: Lumped models become limited; use distributed/S-parameter/field solver
Range_or_sensitivity: Geometry, losses, radiation and fixtures dominate
Formula_or_method: Switch from lumped to TL/S-parameters

Default_value_for_agent: Require dielectric thickness, Er/Df, copper thickness, etch tolerance, soldermask, impedance tolerance
Range_or_sensitivity: Generic FR-4 values are not enough for production
Formula_or_method: Use controlled impedance stack-up from fab

Default_value_for_agent: Do not infer creepage/clearance from EMI source only
Range_or_sensitivity: Use applicable IEC/UL/IPC requirements legally; parasitic model is not safety review
Formula_or_method: Separate EMC parasitic estimate from safety compliance
Inputs_needed: mains voltage, pollution degree, insulation class

Default_value_for_agent: 2.36-15 uH per winding for forward-converter transformers in the studied SMPS; model as a per-winding series inductor
Range_or_sensitivity: Smaller transformer ~2.36 uH, larger ~15 uH; strongly dependent on winding geometry, interleaving and core size. Leakage drives HF ringing that couples into conducted emission.
Formula_or_method: Take from the component datasheet, or measure short-circuit inductance per winding; the paper used datasheet/supplier values rather than generic library parts.
Inputs_needed: transformer part number / datasheet, winding geometry, core size
Use_when: Modelling the SMPS transformer for a conducted-emission time-domain simulation
LTspice_representation: Series inductor on each winding of the coupled-inductor model (K-statement), in addition to the magnetizing inductance

Parasitic: Magnetizing inductance (LM), winding copper resistance (RCU), coupling coefficient
Default_value_for_agent: LM ~1.24-6.4 mH; winding RCU ~0.13-0.34 ohm; coupling KTR ~0.49-1.0; core-loss resistance RFE effectively open (~1e18 ohm)
Range_or_sensitivity: LM scales with core size and turns; RCU rises with winding length, falls with wire gauge; low coupling (KTR ~0.49) implies large leakage and stronger HF emission.
Formula_or_method: Datasheet open-circuit (LM) and short-circuit (leakage) inductance; RCU from DC winding resistance. Model static + dynamic behaviour, not a generic library transformer.
Inputs_needed: transformer datasheet, primary/secondary turns, winding DC resistance
Use_when: Building the transformer block of an SMPS conducted-emission model
LTspice_representation: Coupled inductors with K coupling coefficient; series RCU per winding; magnetizing inductance on the primary

Structure: Power cable / harness between the DUT and the LISN (conducted-emission test setup)
Parasitic: Distributed RLCG of the harness (S-parameter / multi-conductor model)
Default_value_for_agent: Model the DUT-to-LISN harness as an S-parameter (Touchstone) block extracted from a 3D field solver; an LC-ladder is an acceptable first-pass approximation.
Range_or_sensitivity: CRITICAL: the extraction must include a third conductor that continues the LISN reference plane as the current-return reference. Without an explicit return path the extracted S-parameters are invalid. The reference conductor spacing should match the actual DUT conductor spacing.
Formula_or_method: 3D S-parameter extraction with DUT-side ports terminated to a plane on the LISN reference potential (not to a floating object); return-conductor orientation matched to real geometry.
Inputs_needed: cable length and routing, conductor spacing, LISN reference-plane geometry
LTspice_representation: S-parameter block, or the existing CABLE_PWR LC-ladder subcircuit as a first approximation

Structure: PCB layout nets carrying high-frequency switching current
Default_value_for_agent: Extract parasitic RLCG only for nets inside the high-frequency switching current loops (di/dt loops); exclude the rest of the layout from the 3D model to cut solve time without losing emission-relevant accuracy.
Range_or_sensitivity: Paths connected to multiple elements must be split into per-segment sections (e.g. ferrite-to-drain and drain-to-input-cap) so RLCG is extracted per relevant trace. Over-simplification risks missing emission-relevant coupling.
Formula_or_method: Identify the di/dt loops from the converter switching cycles; assign nets, sinks and sources per supposed current-flow direction; extract full RLCG matrices.
Inputs_needed: PCB layout (ODB++/Gerber), the switching current-loop topology
Use_when: Deciding which traces to 3D-extract for a conducted-emission model
LTspice_representation: Per-segment R+L+C (TRACE_RLC) instances spliced into the switching loop

Parasitic: Frequency range over which extracted RLCG / S-parameters are valid
Default_value_for_agent: For a conducted-emission analysis spanning 9 kHz-30 MHz, run the 3D parasitic extraction over 0 Hz-100 MHz with an interpolating sweep; the margin above 30 MHz avoids band-edge error.
Range_or_sensitivity: Quasi-static low-frequency models should start at 0 Hz; submodels should carry both low- and high-frequency margin. Error tolerance ~0.01% in the studied work.
Formula_or_method: Interpolating frequency sweep (faster than discrete); mesh/solution frequency set well above the analysis band (e.g. 1 GHz).
Use_when: Configuring a 3D extractor (Q3D/HFSS) to produce parasitics for time-domain conducted-emission simulation
LTspice_representation: n/a - governs how the extracted RLCG/S-parameter models are produced before import

Structure: 3D field solver selection for parasitic extraction
Default_value_for_agent: Quasi-static solvers are valid for structures up to ~lambda/10; full-wave solvers up to ~lambda/2 or smaller. At 100 MHz lambda ~= 3 m, so quasi-static is valid up to ~0.3 m structures.
Range_or_sensitivity: Both solver classes were accurate enough to extract conducted-emission equivalent circuits in the studied work; choose by available memory / compute time, not accuracy alone.
Formula_or_method: Compare the largest structure dimension against lambda = c/f at the analysis end frequency; pick quasi-static if the dimension < lambda/10.
Inputs_needed: largest layout / harness dimension, analysis end frequency
Use_when: Choosing between a quasi-static and a full-wave extractor for layout / cable parasitics
LTspice_representation: n/a - solver-selection guidance for the extraction step
